overview: Mono-White Heroic in Pauper leverages the Heroic keyword to generate incremental value and board presence while applying steady pressure. By equipping cheap artifacts and casting creatures that trigger Heroic, the deck aims to outpace opponents before they can stabilize. The strategy relies on efficient resource usage and relentless attrition to secure victory.
win_conditions: Wearing down the opponent's life total through repeated Heroic triggers and equipped creatures
Establishing an unblockable or heavily buffed threat that closes out the game
Outlasting the opponent through efficient card advantage and board control

keep_criteria: Hands with early creatures that can trigger Heroic
Hands containing cheap equipment or +1/+1 counters
Hands with a mix of lands and one-drop threats
Keep hands with 2-3 lands and at least one cheap creature (e.g., Knight of the White Orchid) paired with a cheap enchantment (Ethereal Armor, Emerge Unscathed, or Hyena Umbra).
Keep 3+ land hands only if they contain two complete creature-enchantment pairs.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or land drops
Hands heavy on late-game cards without early interaction
Hands with multiple lands or zero creatures
Mulligan hands with 4+ lands, pure land hands, or expensive spells without early threats.

early_game: Play lands and one-drop creatures to establish board presence
Trigger Heroic immediately with cheap equipment or spells
Apply pressure while searching for key synergies
Turn 1: Play a Plains, cast a cheap creature.
Turn 2: Play a Plains, cast a cheap enchantment targeting your creature to trigger Heroic for +1/+1, lifelink, or flying.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast the enchantment the same turn or immediately after.
Prioritize playing creatures that can attack or set up for a pump turn.
mid_game: Scale threats using Emerge Unscathed and Ethereal Armor
Maintain board control by trading efficiently
Look for combat tricks to push damage through
Reserve mana for casting enchantments on your creatures
avoid overcommitting to attacks without protection.
Cast one enchantment per creature per turn to maximize Heroic triggers.
Use your hand efficiently and leverage land-fetching creatures for mana acceleration.
Commit threats when you have at least one enchantment providing protection (e.g., Ethereal Armor for hexproof, Hyena Umbra for flying).
Hold back if the opponent has board wipes or targeted removal and you lack answers.
late_game: Consolidate board state with larger threats
Use accumulated +1/+1 counters to overwhelm blockers
Close out the game with direct damage or unblockable attackers
A fully pumped creature with flying, lifelink, and protection.
Achieve this by chaining multiple enchantments on a single creature, then attacking.
Use creatures that generate card advantage or mana to fuel the final push.
Go all-in when you have a lethal threat (typically 5+ power with flying/lifelink) and the opponent has no clear answer.
Also commit when you're ahead in life and can close the game in 1-2 turns.

key_cards.name: Ethereal Armor
key_cards.role: Provides a cheap way to add +1/+1 counters and trigger Heroic
key_cards.notes: Cast on creatures that can immediately trigger Heroic or will survive to grow

key_cards.name: Emerge Unscathed
key_cards.role: Generates multiple +1/+1 counters and triggers Heroic repeatedly
key_cards.notes: Use when you have multiple creatures on board to maximize value and board presence

key_cards.name: Hyena Umbra
key_cards.role: Offers a resilient early threat with lifelink and evasion potential
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting early to trigger Heroic and apply steady pressure while gaining life

sequencing_tips: Cast equipment or counter-granting spells after declaring attackers to maximize Heroic triggers
Hold up mana for combat tricks to surprise opponents and push unexpected damage
Prioritize keeping creatures alive to compound +1/+1 counters over multiple turns
Heroic triggers only when the enchantment targets only your creature
Be mindful of summoning sickness when attacking.
Enchantments survive board wipes, allowing quick rebuilds.
Watch for flicker effects that can reset your creature's counters.
In unknown formats, verify card legality and adjust sideboard accordingly.

matchups.advice: Play defensively early and use Heroic triggers to stabilize
watch_for: Board wipes that clear your counters

matchups.advice: Push damage quickly before they establish counterspells
watch_for: Card draw engines that outvalue you

matchups.advice: Trade efficiently and avoid giving them free turns
watch_for: Large threats that outscale your board
Removal spells that target your key creatures

matchups.advice: Cast enchantments at end step to avoid counters, or use cheap creatures to bait them. If possible, cast them in response to their spells or use instant-speed options.
Apply early pressure to race them. Use protection enchantments to survive their early threats. If they get ahead, use your enchantments to stabilize and out-value them.
Use your early creatures to trade efficiently. Cast protection enchantments to survive their burst. If you stabilize, transition to your own aggressive plan.
Pressure them with early threats. Cast enchantments to grow your creatures and force them to use removal. If they sweep, use your enchantments to rebuild quickly.
Win the value race by casting more enchantments per turn. Track their creature count and prioritize attacking with your largest threat. Use targeted removal to eliminate their key creatures.

common_threats: Direct damage spells that bypass your blockers
Counterspells that disrupt your key plays
Watch for board wipes, targeted removal on your pumped creatures, and counterspells.
Note if the opponent is holding up mana for removal or committing to a combo/aggro plan.
Track their enchantment count in mirrors to gauge your value race.
